title: Quorum Queues and Replicated Delivery Claims · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs quorum queues and replicated delivery claims from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Quorum Use Case Fit · changed case · Review Quorum Use Case Fit
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 5759 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to compute majority availability while separating quorum custody from stakeholder effect; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Majority Loss Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieving Majority Loss Recovery through the concrete a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Majority Loss Recovery, keep Majority Loss Recovery connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Majority Loss Recovery, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Majority Loss Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Majority Loss Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 8092 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to compute majority availability while separating quorum custody from stakeholder effect; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Majority Loss Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority. In Go, persistent delivery mode alone proves replicated durable storage.
